research_1_spectral_regularization.finding: Spectral regularization improves analog robustness on complex datasets
research_1_spectral_regularization.iris_ceiling: All strategies reach 1.0 robustness on simple task
research_1_spectral_regularization.conclusion: Spectral regularization is effective for complex tasks; combined approach hurts

research_2_transformer_extension.finding: Transformers are naturally robust to analog non-idealities
research_2_transformer_extension.conclusion: Transformers maintain robustness with depth and moderate sparsity

research_3_large_scale.finding: Phase transition confirmed at scale on full MNIST (10k)
research_3_large_scale.cifar10_status: Download too slow (170MB), using synthetic fallback
research_3_large_scale.conclusion: Phase transition and sparse robustness scale to larger datasets

research_4_transformer_robustness.finding: Transformers outperform MLPs on ALL non-idealities; residuals NOT the cause
research_4_transformer_robustness.ablation_residual_effect: Residual connections HURT MLP (-0.148 to -0.200) - robustness not from skip connections
research_4_transformer_robustness.ablation_attention: Attention mechanism = key to transformer robustness
research_4_transformer_robustness.conclusion: Attention-based computation is inherently more robust to analog errors than feedforward computation. This is a novel discovery.

research_5_spice_validation.finding: FallbackNodalSolver produces 100% identical results to reference PyTorch forward pass
research_5_spice_validation.pyspice_status: Available, falls back to analytical solver
